metadata.seed: Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I do feel a bit all over the place. I really care about Stu, but then I hear things from my family or friends that just get to me. It’s like... one moment, everything feels right, and the next, I’m questioning everything. I don’t know if I’m just being foolish or if they really have a point.

patient.query: Thanks, I appreciate that. It’s just... when I’m with Stu, things feel good. But then I think about what my mom said the other day, about how I should be focusing more on my career instead of relationships. It makes me wonder if I’m wasting my time or if I’m being selfish. I can’t seem to shake that doubt.

patient.query: I guess when I think about being selfish, it’s just... I have this nagging feeling that maybe I’m putting my own happiness above other important things, like my job and my future. Sometimes I hear my friends talk about their goals and ambitions, and I can’t help but feel like I’m just... floating along. Like I’m not doing enough, you know? It makes me feel guilty.

patient.query: I don't know, I just feel like I should have everything figured out by now. It’s like everyone else has their life together, and I’m just here... I guess I fear that if I focus too much on my relationship, I’ll end up regretting it later, or letting my family down. I don’t want to be that person who just ignores what really matters. But then, when I’m with Stu, it really feels like what matters is right there. It’s confusing.

patient.query: I... I think what really matters is feeling fulfilled and happy, but it’s hard to pinpoint how to get there. With Stu, I do feel happy, but then there’s this voice in my head that reminds me of my responsibilities and what I “should” be doing. It’s like I feel torn between wanting to enjoy this relationship and the fear that I’m not being responsible enough or living up to expectations. It just feels like I can’t settle into one thing without feeling guilty about the other.
